title: thymus serpyllum f. citriodorus
intro: Thymus serpyllum f. citriodorus, also known as lemon thyme, is a fragrant, low-growing perennial plant native to Europe, known for its lemon-scented leaves and delicate purple flowers. The medicinal properties of lemon thyme include its antiseptic, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which make it effective in treating wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of lemon thyme include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce stress and anxiety, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu, making it a popular herbal remedy for respiratory issues. Lemon thyme can be consumed as a tea, used as an essential oil in aromatherapy, or added to food as a culinary herb, making it a versatile and useful plant for medicinal and culinary purposes.
